A conservative, targeted fix is to only consider stores for grouping when both pointer operands are from inbounds GEPs (or exactly the same base pointer). This avoids merging widely separated or wrapping addresses without broadly disabling store vectorization.

```bash
python3 - << 'PY'
import re,sys
p='llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/SLPVectorizer.cpp'
s=open(p).read()
# Inject conservative inbounds GEP guard into RelatedStoreInsts::getPointerDiff.
pat = r'(std::optional<int>\s+getPointerDiff\s*\(\s*StoreInst\s*&SI,\s*const\s*DataLayout\s*&DL,\s*ScalarEvolution\s*&SE\s*\)\s*const\s*\{\s*StoreInst\s*&BaseStore\s*=\s*\*AllStores\[BaseInstrIdx\];\s*)'
def repl(m):
    head = m.group(1)
    guard = (
        '  Value *BasePtr = BaseStore.getPointerOperand();\n'
        '  Value *SIPtr = SI.getPointerOperand();\n'
        '  auto *BaseGEP = dyn_cast<GetElementPtrInst>(BasePtr);\n'
        '  auto *SIGEP = dyn_cast<GetElementPtrInst>(SIPtr);\n'
        '  if (!(BasePtr == SIPtr) && (!(BaseGEP && BaseGEP->isInBounds()) || !(SIGEP && SIGEP->isInBounds())))\n'
        '    return std::nullopt;\n'
    )
    return head + guard
s2, n = re.subn(pat, repl, s, flags=re.S)
if n == 0:
    print("Failed to locate getPointerDiff for patch", file=sys.stderr); sys.exit(1)
open(p,'w').write(s2)
print("Patched inbounds guard in getPointerDiff")
PY && submit-patch
```
